item.title: Score our favorite AMD Ryzen CPU for cheap as it falls to its lowest price yet
item.itunes:title: Score our favorite AMD Ryzen CPU for cheap as it falls to its lowest price yet
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
AMD Ryzen 5 8600G
$182 $229 Save $47

The AMD Ryzen 5 8600G is an affordable APU with integrated graphics processing. This allows you to play games without a dedicated GPU with good results at 1080p.

$182 at Amazon

There are a lot of great options when it comes to CPUs, but if you're looking to build a new gaming rig and want a processor that's not going to break the bank but still offers lots of performance, then the AMD Ryzen 5 8600G is going to be for you. Not only does this CPU deliver performance above its price tag, it can now be had for far less than its original price, with a stellar discount that drops it down to its lowest price yet.

Related
AMD Ryzen 5 8600G review: The only affordable gaming CPU you should buy

Amazing 1080p performance on a budget

For a limited time, you can score this Ryzen 5 8600G for just $182, which is 21% less than its current retail price. When we originally reviewed this CPU, we stated that "it was the only affordable gaming CPU you should buy." And that still holds true today, especially at its newly discounted price. So get this deal while you can because it won't last long.

What's great about AMD's Ryzen 5 8600G?

What makes the Ryzen 5 8600G such a great choice is not only its price, but also its performance as well. It comes with integrated graphics, which means, if you're on a budget, you won't need to purchase a dedicated graphics card to play games. As far as what you're getting, this AM5 processor has 6 cores with 12 threads, and features a base clock speed of 4.3Ghz that can top out at 5Ghz.

If all of that wasn't enough, as stated before, you're also going to get integrated graphics here as well, which is on par with a Radeon 760M with 8 GPU cores. This processor also comes with a bundled cooler, which isn't the best, but it will still get the job done. In our testing, we saw popular games like Elden Ring hit close to 60 frames per second, while oldies like GTA V peaked at 90fps.

So long as you're looking to game at 1080p, this APU is really going to blow you away with its performance and fantastic price. So, if you've been thinking about building a new PC without a dedicated graphics card and want something that's not going to break the bank, the Ryzen 5 8600G is going to be the one to get.

item.title: Best RTX 4090 laptops in 2024
item.itunes:title: Best RTX 4090 laptops in 2024
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Buying a high-end laptop with a Nvidia RTX 4090 graphics card inside generally means that you're aiming for the best performance possible for gaming or for other intensive tasks like 3D rendering. Nvidia's RTX 4090 Laptop GPU won't match the performance of its desktop counterpart due to heat and power constraints, but it will still offer the best mobile power around. If you're interested in taking your gaming PC everywhere and don't want to lag far behind a desktop setup, one of these laptops should be the way to go. I've been sure to specify the RTX 4090's TGP specific to each model to ensure you're not getting a card that's held back by power constraints.

  • Source: Lenovo
    Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9
    Editor's Choice
    $2699 at B&H
  • Razer Blade 18 (2024)
    Premium Pick
    $4500 at Best Buy
  • Asus ROG Strix Scar 16 (2024)
    Mini-LED display
    $3644 at Amazon
  • Alienware M18 R2 Gaming Laptop
    Best 18-inch
    $3150 at Dell
  • Lenovo Legion 9i (2024)
    Liquid cooling
    $3299 at B&H
  • Source: Dell
    Alienware x16 R2
    Slim design
    $3400 at Dell
  • Source: Asus
    Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 (2024)
    Best OLED laptop
    $3000 at Best Buy

Our favorite laptops with Nvidia's RTX 4090 GPU

Editor's Choice
Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9

The right mix for most people

9 / 10
$2699 $3299 Save $600

The Lenovo Legion Pro 7i is our favorite overall gaming laptop, and it happens to be available with up to an RTX 4090 laptop GPU running at a 175W TGP. It also has a 14th Gen Intel Core i9-14900HX CPU, a 16-inch QHD+ display, and up to 32GB of DDR5 RAM out of the box.

Pros
  • High-end performance that doesn't overheat
  • Premium design and build quality
  • Many display and config options
Cons
  • Relatively heavy
  • No 4K display option
$3145 at Lenovo $2699 at B&H $3619 at Adorama

When it comes to the overall best gaming laptops available today, the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9 remains our top pick with the 2024 model. It's not absolutely perfect, but it's easy to look past the so-so battery life (it is a gaming laptop after all) and lack of 4K display option when confronted with all the great features. The build quality is outstanding, and the design is fairly subdued with just the right amount of flair. It has a load of ports along the back for easy cable management, there's a 1080p webcam, the speakers offer quality sound, and there's plenty of customizable RGB lighting.

The 14th-gen Intel Core i9-14900HX processor (CPU) has the power to rival a desktop setup, and it's joined by up to 32GB of DDR5 RAM and 2TB of M.2 PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSD storage split between two drives. Opting for an RTX 4090 laptop GPU brings the price up to well over $3,000, but that's got to be expected if you're aiming for this kind of performance. It has a whopping 175W of power, too, the maximum allowed by Nvidia's design.

Related
Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9 review: An already-great laptop gets a spec bump and display upgrade

With a chip upgrade, an optional Nvidia RTX 4090 GPU, and a more color-accurate display, one of our favorite gaming laptops just got better.

Our main issue with last year's model of the Legion Pro 7i was the display, but for the 2024 edition, Lenovo fixed it and gave us a fantastic screen that covers 99% of DCI-P3, in addition to having Quad HD resolution and a 240Hz refresh rate. In the words of our reviewer, Brady Snyder, "this subtle display upgrade makes the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9 an even more well-rounded gaming laptop", and so, it's easier than ever to recommend to just about anyone.

Premium Pick
Razer Blade 18 (2024)

Razer's desktop replacement

The Blade 18 is the biggest and most powerful gaming laptop from Razer. It's incredibly expensive, but it represents the pinnacle of modern laptop design. It's packed with high-end features, including a 14th Gen Intel Core i9, an RTX 4090 with 175W of power, and a Quad HD+ Mini-LED display with a 300Hz refresh rate.

Pros
  • The sleekest 18-inch powerhouse around
  • Top-tier 14th-gen Intel Core i9 and RTX 4090
  • Stunning Mini-LED 18-inch QHD+ display at 300Hz
Cons
  • No 4K display option
  • Extremely expensive
$4500 at Best Buy $4500 at Amazon $4500 at Razer

The 2024 Razer Blade 18 is once again one of the absolute best gaming laptops money can buy. Despite the dimensions — the laptop measures just 0.86 inches (21.9mm) thin — Razer allows the Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 to run at up to a 175W TGP for maximum mobile performance. That RTX 4090 is paired with a top-of-the-line Intel Core i9-14900HX processor, along with uip to 64GB of RAM and 4TB of SSD storage.

Related
The Razer Blade 18 is the world's first laptop with Thunderbolt 5 and a 4K 200Hz display

Razer has finally launched the Blade 18, and it's looking like the ultimate machine — with a price tag to match.

The 18-inch display got a big upgrade for the 2024 refresh, too. You now get a Mini-LED panel with a super-smooth 300Hz refresh rate, while keeping the same Quad HD+ resolution. That effectively makes this one of the best laptop display around for gaming. Alternatively, you can get a 4K 200Hz panel, which is mind-blowing in its own way, since it's also unheard of on a laptop. That display sits below a 5MP webcam that's basically unheard of in a gaming laptop, complete with Windows Hello support.

Razer really pulled out all the stops, even making this the first-ever laptop to support Thunderbolt 5, so you're very future-proof, too. Per-key RGB lighting for the keyboard is customizable, the touchpad is huge, there are lots of ports, and the black finish can fit in pretty much anywhere. The biggest issue with these laptops is the price. A model with the RTX 4090 GPU will cost you at least $4,500 unless you can find it on sale. But you're getting a lot for that money.

Mini-LED display
Asus ROG Strix Scar 16 (2024)

16-inch Nebula QHD display

8 / 10

Craving the majesty of a mini-LED display? The Asus ROG Strix Scar 16 brings a gorgeous 240Hz Nebula HDR screen with 240Hz refresh rate and 1,100 nits brightness with over 2,000 local dimming zones. This model features an Intel Core i9-14900HX CPU and RTX 4090 laptop GPU for ultimate power.

Pros
  • Mini-LED display is incredible
  • Intel Core i9-13980HX CPU and RTX 4090 GPU running at 175W
  • Modern design, lots of RGB lighting
Cons
  • No 4K resolution
$3644 at Amazon $3645 at Best Buy $3700 at Asus

Some of the best laptops with mini-LED display are designed for high-end PC gaming, and that's exactly where the Asus ROG Strix Scar 16 falls into place. The focus here really should be on the 16-inch Nebula HDR mini-LED display. It has a 2560x1600 (QHD+) resolution to match the 16:10 aspect ratio, it hits 100% DCI-P3 color, it has a sizzling 240Hz refresh rate and 3ms response time, and it has G-Sync baked right in. The mini-LED tech allows for 2,000 local dimming zones (nearly four times the prvious model) for outstanding color and contrast, with 1,100 nits brightness at peak and VESA DisplayHDR 1000/Dolby Vision HDR compatibility.

It will be tough to go back to a regular display after using mini-LED, but the laptop should last for years thanks to its incredible performance. It packs in a 14th-gen Intel Core i9-14900HX CPU and a Nvidia RTX 4090 laptop GPU running at 175W. Considering it also has 32GB of DDR5 RAM and 2TB of M.2 PCIe NVMe SSD storage, the $3,500 price tag makes a lot of sense, and it just shows you're paying for an incredible product.

Related
Asus ROG Strix Scar 16 (2024) review: Big on both performance and price

A powerful machine that looks unique with its semi-transparent design.

Thunderbolt 4 and HDMI 2.1 are on board for modern connectivity, and you get per-key RGB lighting for the keyboard. Keys have a deep 2mm travel for extra comfort. A light bar along the front can also be synced up for extra RGB. There's only a 720p camera here, but there is a quad-speaker setup with Dolby Atmos for quality audio. Overall, this laptop should prove to be quite popular for those who value a great built-in display and RTX 4090 performance without overspending.

Best 18-inch
Alienware M18 R2 Gaming Laptop

Big screen, big performance

If you want a big screen, it's hard to do much better than the Alienware m18 R2. This beastly 18-inch laptop packs top-tier Intel processors and up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090. It also has a bvariety of display configurations for different tastes, and it looks great to boot.

Pros
  • Large 18-inch display with great colors and configuration options
  • Full-power RTX 4090 with 175W of power
  • Plenty of ports and an optional mechanical keyboard
Cons
  • Quad HD+ display is limited to 165Hz
$3150 at Dell $3800 at Best Buy

Gaming is much more immersive with a huge screen, and the closest you're going to get on a laptop is 18 inches. And if you want the best 18-inch laptop around, the Alienware m18 R2 is for you. As you can expect, this is a beastly machine, featuring 14th-gen Intel processors up to a Core i9-14900HX, and up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 running at 175W, the maximum allowed by the GPU itself. You can also kit it out with up to 64GB of DDR5 RAM and an 8TB of SSD storage.

There are a couple of major display options here, too, with either a Quad HD+ panel running at 165Hz or a super-smooth Full HD+ display that can go up to 480Hz if you value fluidity above all else. Both options cover 100% of DCI-P3, so colors look great either way.

Related
Alienware m18 R2 review: A premium, beastly laptop for no-compromises gaming

Last year's Alienware m18 was already good, but the m18 R2 and its 14th-gen HX processor and RTX 4090 graphics is excellent.

The real treat is the option for a CherryMX mechanical keyboard, which offers a fantastic feel unlike anything you can get on other laptops. If you've used one of these keyboards, you know just how satisfying they feel, and it's simply fantastic. On top of the display you also get a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support, which is always great to see.

Otherwise, the Alienware m18 R2 is a simply great-looking laptop. There's a bit of RGB with the keyboard and the ring light on the back, making it flashy if you're into that, and there are a lot of ports for all your peripherals. It's simply a fantastic laptop if you want something big.

Liquid cooling
Lenovo Legion 9i (2024)

Maximum performance in a thin chassis

$3299 $4399 Save $1100

The Lenovo Legion 9i breaks all the conventions by packing a true liquid cooling loop into its 16-inch chassis, extracting top-notch performance from its 14th-gen Intel processor and RTX 4090 GPU. It also has a fantastic mini-LED display with Quad HD+ resolution and a 240Hz refresh rate.

Pros
  • Top-tier Intel Core i9 processor and RTX 4090 GPU
  • Liquid cooling loop maximizes performance in a more compact chassis
  • Unique design with plenty of RGB
Cons
  • Very pricy
$3620 at Lenovo $3299 at B&H

When we reviewed the 2023 Lenovo Legion 9i, it was one of the best gaming laptops money could buy, if not the best. Lenovo has come back for round two with this model, upgrading the performance with the new Intel Core i9-14900HX, which is still paired with the incredibly powerful RTX 4090 GPU running at 175W. Lenovo also has a dedicated AI chip to help improve performance even more. What really seals the deal, though, is that this is the only 16-inch laptop to have a proper liquid cooling loop inside of it, which allows it to run cooler while being much thinner than comparable laptops.

Related
Lenovo Legion 9i (2024): Everything to know about this flagship gaming laptop

Lenovo's best gaming laptop is even better in 2024 thanks to its supercharged AI features that can boost the framerates in your games.

On top of that, you get an excellent display here, with a 16-inch Mmini-LED panel with Quad HD+ resolution and a 240hz refresh rate. it delivers up to 1,200 nits of brightness for an incredible HDR experience in games, and the colors look just as vivid as you could want them to.

Add to that a cool and comfortable RGB keyboard, plenty of RGB lighting, and a unique carbon chip design on the lid, and you have one of the most unique laptops around with some of the best performance money can buy. You also get an array of ports on the back, a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support, and a nice touchpad, so this is the full package, even if it comes at a high cost.

Slim design
Alienware x16 R2

Sleek looks with lots of power

9 / 10

The Alienware x16 R2 has a standout style with plenty of RGB lighting and a thin design considering its powerful specs. Its Intel Core Ultra processors can be paired with an RTX 4090 laptop GPU running at 175W. Choose from 16-inch QHD+ displays with 165Hz or 240Hz refresh rate, and enjoy the optional CherryMX mechanical keyboard.

Pros
  • Sleek design with a ton of RGB, including an optional light-up touchpad
  • Killer 16-inch QHD+ display at 240Hz
  • Top-tier GPU performance despite the compact chassis
Cons
  • No 4K display option
  • Availability is more limited than some other models
$3400 at Dell

Dell's Alienware brand has always stood out to me thanks to its unique design cues and exceptional performance, and the Alienware x16 R2 is the best yet. The rounded back edge offers some exhaust venting and a port bank, surrounded by a customizable RGB lighting ring. A honeycomb grille lives between the keyboard and display, the black interior offsets the exterior quite nicely, and the touchpad can even light up with RGB. Dell has gone with a low-profile keyboard using CherryMX switches for that mechanical feel and performance. A 1080p webcam lives above the display for quality video streaming, and there's even an IR sensor for Windows Hello.

The laptop measures just 0.73 inches (18.57mm) thin, making it one of the thinner RTX 4090 gaming laptops out there today. The GPU can still hit the maximum 175W TGP, though instead of using an HX-series processor, Dell adopted an Intel Core Ultra chip instead, so CPU performance isn't as powerful as some other models (though on the flip side, it has some AI capabilities).

2:09
Related
Alienware x16 R2 review: This gaming laptop is more powerful than ever — most of the time

This gaming laptop is still full of RGB and great aesthetics, but now includes Intel Core Ultra and other upgrades inside.

The 16-inch display comes with a 16:10 aspect ratio and Quad HD+ resolution with a 240Hz refresh rate, or a Full HD+ panel with a 480Hz refresh rate depending on what you want to prioritize. Both panels are pretty good, covering 100% of DCI-P3, and in the words of our reviewer Brady Snyder, "the Alienware x16 R2 can keep up with the most color-accurate gaming laptops out there".

Aside from the essentials, you get plenty of ports for such a thin laptop, plus there's a ton of RGB, with the keyboard, ring light on the back, and even an optional light-up touchpad that's way more flashy than it needed to be. It's really fun laptop to show off, on top of just being a great one.

Best OLED laptop
Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 (2024)

A stunning display in a sleek chassis

8.5 / 10
$3000 $3300 Save $300

The Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 is an even sleeker option if you want a gaming machine that's more portable but still powerful. It has an Intel Core 9 processor and up to an RTX 4090 GPU running at 115W. It's also got a stunning OLED display that's even better than mini-LED,

Pros
  • Stunning OLED display offers absolutely stunning visuals and HDR
  • Thin and relatively light design considering the specs
  • Great speaker setup and a large touchpad
Cons
  • GPU is less powerful than larger laptops
  • It can get pretty hot
$3000 at Best Buy $3500 at Amazon $3300 at Asus

Mini-LED displays are great, but if you want the best visual experience, OLED is the way to go, and Asus is happy to provide with the ROG Zephyrus G16. This 16-inch laptop packs a stunning OLED display with 500 nits of brightness in HDR mode, offering one of the best HDR experiences you can get. The individually-lit pixels are even better than mini-LED, whether you're gaming, working, or watching movies.

It's a pretty fast laptop, too. Due to the thin design, it comes with an Intel Core Ultra 9 185H processor rather than the more power-hungry HX-series chips, but it's still plenty fast. You also get the option for an RTX 4090, though it's limited to 115W of power. This is a consequence of the thinner design, and it's important to note this is the lightest laptop on this list. You lose some performance, but you get some portability in return, so it's all about picking your priorities.

Related
Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 (2024) review: Major redesign makes this laptop great for more than just gaming

The new ROG Zephyrus G16 is a premium laptop with mature design, appealing to anyone who wants to work and game on the same device.

Even with its smaller chassis, the ROG Zephyrus G16 has an excellent keyboard with plenty of travel, a large touchpad, and solid speakers that easily drown out fan noise. You also get a solid selection of ports, and on top of that, it's all wrapped in a very sleek design that barely gives away this is a gaming laptop. This is a laptop you can use anywhere, and as a result, it can be great for almost anyone.

In our review, Cale Hunt said "The ROG Zephryus G16 (2024) is one of the best built laptops I've tested, with a high attention to detail evident everywhere you look", and that's not overselling it. This is a great choice.

Getting the best RTX 4090 laptop for you

Any of the laptops that I've listed above come with an option for the mighty Nvidia RTX 4090 laptop GPU, capable of delivering the best gaming performance possible before stepping into a desktop setup. Many of these laptops will in fact rival and even best some of the most popular pre-built gaming desktop PCs when it comes to raw power, and of course you get a built-in display and many other extra features that make your life with the laptop as easy as possible. None of these laptops are affordable, but that 's to be expected if you want an RTX 4090.

These are all great laptops, but our top pick has to go to the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9. It's our favorite overall gaming laptop right now, and it just so happens to be configurable with up to a Nvidia RTX 4090 laptop GPU running at the full 175W potential. The build quality and design and outstanding, there are many configuration options, and the QHD display is a beauty (even if it doesn't have the same pixel density as 4K).

Any of the other options has its own novelty, whether that's mini-LED, liquid cooling, or a thinner and more portable design. There's something for everyone here.

Editor's Choice
Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9
9 / 10
$2699 $3299 Save $600

With a powerful 14th-gen Intel i9 chip, up to an Nvidia RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, and up to 32GB of DDR5 RAM, the Legion Pro 7i all-around extremely capable gaming laptop. Throw in a 240Hz G-SYNC display and you've got a seriously competitive machine.

$3145 at Lenovo $2699 at B&H $3619 at Adorama

Related

With a chip upgrade, an optional Nvidia RTX 4090 GPU, and a more color-accurate display, one of our favorite gaming laptops just got better.

Our main issue with last year's model of the Legion Pro 7i was the display, but for the 2024 edition, Lenovo fixed it and gave us a fantastic screen that covers 99% of DCI-P3, in addition to having Quad HD resolution and a 240Hz refresh rate. In the words of our reviewer, Brady Snyder, "this subtle display upgrade makes the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9 an even more well-rounded gaming laptop", and so, it's easier than ever to recommend to just about anyone.

Premium Pick
Razer Blade 18 (2024)

Razer's desktop replacement

The Blade 18 is the biggest and most powerful gaming laptop from Razer. It's incredibly expensive, but it represents the pinnacle of modern laptop design. It's packed with high-end features, including a 14th Gen Intel Core i9, an RTX 4090 with 175W of power, and a Quad HD+ Mini-LED display with a 300Hz refresh rate.

Pros
  • The sleekest 18-inch powerhouse around
  • Top-tier 14th-gen Intel Core i9 and RTX 4090
  • Stunning Mini-LED 18-inch QHD+ display at 300Hz
Cons
  • No 4K display option
  • Extremely expensive
$4500 at Best Buy $4500 at Amazon $4500 at Razer

The 2024 Razer Blade 18 is once again one of the absolute best gaming laptops money can buy. Despite the dimensions — the laptop measures just 0.86 inches (21.9mm) thin — Razer allows the Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 to run at up to a 175W TGP for maximum mobile performance. That RTX 4090 is paired with a top-of-the-line Intel Core i9-14900HX processor, along with uip to 64GB of RAM and 4TB of SSD storage.

Related
The Razer Blade 18 is the world's first laptop with Thunderbolt 5 and a 4K 200Hz display

Razer has finally launched the Blade 18, and it's looking like the ultimate machine — with a price tag to match.

The 18-inch display got a big upgrade for the 2024 refresh, too. You now get a Mini-LED panel with a super-smooth 300Hz refresh rate, while keeping the same Quad HD+ resolution. That effectively makes this one of the best laptop display around for gaming. Alternatively, you can get a 4K 200Hz panel, which is mind-blowing in its own way, since it's also unheard of on a laptop. That display sits below a 5MP webcam that's basically unheard of in a gaming laptop, complete with Windows Hello support.

Razer really pulled out all the stops, even making this the first-ever laptop to support Thunderbolt 5, so you're very future-proof, too. Per-key RGB lighting for the keyboard is customizable, the touchpad is huge, there are lots of ports, and the black finish can fit in pretty much anywhere. The biggest issue with these laptops is the price. A model with the RTX 4090 GPU will cost you at least $4,500 unless you can find it on sale. But you're getting a lot for that money.

Mini-LED display
Asus ROG Strix Scar 16 (2024)

16-inch Nebula QHD display

8 / 10

Craving the majesty of a mini-LED display? The Asus ROG Strix Scar 16 brings a gorgeous 240Hz Nebula HDR screen with 240Hz refresh rate and 1,100 nits brightness with over 2,000 local dimming zones. This model features an Intel Core i9-14900HX CPU and RTX 4090 laptop GPU for ultimate power.

Pros
  • Mini-LED display is incredible
  • Intel Core i9-13980HX CPU and RTX 4090 GPU running at 175W
  • Modern design, lots of RGB lighting
Cons
  • No 4K resolution
$3644 at Amazon $3645 at Best Buy $3700 at Asus

Some of the best laptops with mini-LED display are designed for high-end PC gaming, and that's exactly where the Asus ROG Strix Scar 16 falls into place. The focus here really should be on the 16-inch Nebula HDR mini-LED display. It has a 2560x1600 (QHD+) resolution to match the 16:10 aspect ratio, it hits 100% DCI-P3 color, it has a sizzling 240Hz refresh rate and 3ms response time, and it has G-Sync baked right in. The mini-LED tech allows for 2,000 local dimming zones (nearly four times the prvious model) for outstanding color and contrast, with 1,100 nits brightness at peak and VESA DisplayHDR 1000/Dolby Vision HDR compatibility.

It will be tough to go back to a regular display after using mini-LED, but the laptop should last for years thanks to its incredible performance. It packs in a 14th-gen Intel Core i9-14900HX CPU and a Nvidia RTX 4090 laptop GPU running at 175W. Considering it also has 32GB of DDR5 RAM and 2TB of M.2 PCIe NVMe SSD storage, the $3,500 price tag makes a lot of sense, and it just shows you're paying for an incredible product.

Related
Asus ROG Strix Scar 16 (2024) review: Big on both performance and price

A powerful machine that looks unique with its semi-transparent design.

Thunderbolt 4 and HDMI 2.1 are on board for modern connectivity, and you get per-key RGB lighting for the keyboard. Keys have a deep 2mm travel for extra comfort. A light bar along the front can also be synced up for extra RGB. There's only a 720p camera here, but there is a quad-speaker setup with Dolby Atmos for quality audio. Overall, this laptop should prove to be quite popular for those who value a great built-in display and RTX 4090 performance without overspending.

Best 18-inch
Alienware M18 R2 Gaming Laptop

Big screen, big performance

If you want a big screen, it's hard to do much better than the Alienware m18 R2. This beastly 18-inch laptop packs top-tier Intel processors and up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090. It also has a bvariety of display configurations for different tastes, and it looks great to boot.

Pros
  • Large 18-inch display with great colors and configuration options
  • Full-power RTX 4090 with 175W of power
  • Plenty of ports and an optional mechanical keyboard
Cons
  • Quad HD+ display is limited to 165Hz
$3150 at Dell $3800 at Best Buy

Gaming is much more immersive with a huge screen, and the closest you're going to get on a laptop is 18 inches. And if you want the best 18-inch laptop around, the Alienware m18 R2 is for you. As you can expect, this is a beastly machine, featuring 14th-gen Intel processors up to a Core i9-14900HX, and up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 running at 175W, the maximum allowed by the GPU itself. You can also kit it out with up to 64GB of DDR5 RAM and an 8TB of SSD storage.

There are a couple of major display options here, too, with either a Quad HD+ panel running at 165Hz or a super-smooth Full HD+ display that can go up to 480Hz if you value fluidity above all else. Both options cover 100% of DCI-P3, so colors look great either way.

Related
Alienware m18 R2 review: A premium, beastly laptop for no-compromises gaming

Last year's Alienware m18 was already good, but the m18 R2 and its 14th-gen HX processor and RTX 4090 graphics is excellent.

The real treat is the option for a CherryMX mechanical keyboard, which offers a fantastic feel unlike anything you can get on other laptops. If you've used one of these keyboards, you know just how satisfying they feel, and it's simply fantastic. On top of the display you also get a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support, which is always great to see.

Otherwise, the Alienware m18 R2 is a simply great-looking laptop. There's a bit of RGB with the keyboard and the ring light on the back, making it flashy if you're into that, and there are a lot of ports for all your peripherals. It's simply a fantastic laptop if you want something big.

Liquid cooling
Lenovo Legion 9i (2024)

Maximum performance in a thin chassis

$3299 $4399 Save $1100

The Lenovo Legion 9i breaks all the conventions by packing a true liquid cooling loop into its 16-inch chassis, extracting top-notch performance from its 14th-gen Intel processor and RTX 4090 GPU. It also has a fantastic mini-LED display with Quad HD+ resolution and a 240Hz refresh rate.

Pros
  • Top-tier Intel Core i9 processor and RTX 4090 GPU
  • Liquid cooling loop maximizes performance in a more compact chassis
  • Unique design with plenty of RGB
Cons
  • Very pricy
$3620 at Lenovo $3299 at B&H

When we reviewed the 2023 Lenovo Legion 9i, it was one of the best gaming laptops money could buy, if not the best. Lenovo has come back for round two with this model, upgrading the performance with the new Intel Core i9-14900HX, which is still paired with the incredibly powerful RTX 4090 GPU running at 175W. Lenovo also has a dedicated AI chip to help improve performance even more. What really seals the deal, though, is that this is the only 16-inch laptop to have a proper liquid cooling loop inside of it, which allows it to run cooler while being much thinner than comparable laptops.

Related
Lenovo Legion 9i (2024): Everything to know about this flagship gaming laptop

Lenovo's best gaming laptop is even better in 2024 thanks to its supercharged AI features that can boost the framerates in your games.

On top of that, you get an excellent display here, with a 16-inch Mmini-LED panel with Quad HD+ resolution and a 240hz refresh rate. it delivers up to 1,200 nits of brightness for an incredible HDR experience in games, and the colors look just as vivid as you could want them to.

Add to that a cool and comfortable RGB keyboard, plenty of RGB lighting, and a unique carbon chip design on the lid, and you have one of the most unique laptops around with some of the best performance money can buy. You also get an array of ports on the back, a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support, and a nice touchpad, so this is the full package, even if it comes at a high cost.

Slim design
Alienware x16 R2

Sleek looks with lots of power

9 / 10

The Alienware x16 R2 has a standout style with plenty of RGB lighting and a thin design considering its powerful specs. Its Intel Core Ultra processors can be paired with an RTX 4090 laptop GPU running at 175W. Choose from 16-inch QHD+ displays with 165Hz or 240Hz refresh rate, and enjoy the optional CherryMX mechanical keyboard.

Pros
  • Sleek design with a ton of RGB, including an optional light-up touchpad
  • Killer 16-inch QHD+ display at 240Hz
  • Top-tier GPU performance despite the compact chassis
Cons
  • No 4K display option
  • Availability is more limited than some other models
$3400 at Dell

Dell's Alienware brand has always stood out to me thanks to its unique design cues and exceptional performance, and the Alienware x16 R2 is the best yet. The rounded back edge offers some exhaust venting and a port bank, surrounded by a customizable RGB lighting ring. A honeycomb grille lives between the keyboard and display, the black interior offsets the exterior quite nicely, and the touchpad can even light up with RGB. Dell has gone with a low-profile keyboard using CherryMX switches for that mechanical feel and performance. A 1080p webcam lives above the display for quality video streaming, and there's even an IR sensor for Windows Hello.

The laptop measures just 0.73 inches (18.57mm) thin, making it one of the thinner RTX 4090 gaming laptops out there today. The GPU can still hit the maximum 175W TGP, though instead of using an HX-series processor, Dell adopted an Intel Core Ultra chip instead, so CPU performance isn't as powerful as some other models (though on the flip side, it has some AI capabilities).

2:09
Related
Alienware x16 R2 review: This gaming laptop is more powerful than ever — most of the time

This gaming laptop is still full of RGB and great aesthetics, but now includes Intel Core Ultra and other upgrades inside.

The 16-inch display comes with a 16:10 aspect ratio and Quad HD+ resolution with a 240Hz refresh rate, or a Full HD+ panel with a 480Hz refresh rate depending on what you want to prioritize. Both panels are pretty good, covering 100% of DCI-P3, and in the words of our reviewer Brady Snyder, "the Alienware x16 R2 can keep up with the most color-accurate gaming laptops out there".

Aside from the essentials, you get plenty of ports for such a thin laptop, plus there's a ton of RGB, with the keyboard, ring light on the back, and even an optional light-up touchpad that's way more flashy than it needed to be. It's really fun laptop to show off, on top of just being a great one.

Best OLED laptop
Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 (2024)

A stunning display in a sleek chassis

8.5 / 10
$3000 $3300 Save $300

The Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 is an even sleeker option if you want a gaming machine that's more portable but still powerful. It has an Intel Core 9 processor and up to an RTX 4090 GPU running at 115W. It's also got a stunning OLED display that's even better than mini-LED,

Pros
  • Stunning OLED display offers absolutely stunning visuals and HDR
  • Thin and relatively light design considering the specs
  • Great speaker setup and a large touchpad
Cons
  • GPU is less powerful than larger laptops
  • It can get pretty hot
$3000 at Best Buy $3500 at Amazon $3300 at Asus

Mini-LED displays are great, but if you want the best visual experience, OLED is the way to go, and Asus is happy to provide with the ROG Zephyrus G16. This 16-inch laptop packs a stunning OLED display with 500 nits of brightness in HDR mode, offering one of the best HDR experiences you can get. The individually-lit pixels are even better than mini-LED, whether you're gaming, working, or watching movies.

It's a pretty fast laptop, too. Due to the thin design, it comes with an Intel Core Ultra 9 185H processor rather than the more power-hungry HX-series chips, but it's still plenty fast. You also get the option for an RTX 4090, though it's limited to 115W of power. This is a consequence of the thinner design, and it's important to note this is the lightest laptop on this list. You lose some performance, but you get some portability in return, so it's all about picking your priorities.

Related
Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 (2024) review: Major redesign makes this laptop great for more than just gaming

The new ROG Zephyrus G16 is a premium laptop with mature design, appealing to anyone who wants to work and game on the same device.

Even with its smaller chassis, the ROG Zephyrus G16 has an excellent keyboard with plenty of travel, a large touchpad, and solid speakers that easily drown out fan noise. You also get a solid selection of ports, and on top of that, it's all wrapped in a very sleek design that barely gives away this is a gaming laptop. This is a laptop you can use anywhere, and as a result, it can be great for almost anyone.

In our review, Cale Hunt said "The ROG Zephryus G16 (2024) is one of the best built laptops I've tested, with a high attention to detail evident everywhere you look", and that's not overselling it. This is a great choice.

Getting the best RTX 4090 laptop for you

Any of the laptops that I've listed above come with an option for the mighty Nvidia RTX 4090 laptop GPU, capable of delivering the best gaming performance possible before stepping into a desktop setup. Many of these laptops will in fact rival and even best some of the most popular pre-built gaming desktop PCs when it comes to raw power, and of course you get a built-in display and many other extra features that make your life with the laptop as easy as possible. None of these laptops are affordable, but that 's to be expected if you want an RTX 4090.

These are all great laptops, but our top pick has to go to the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9. It's our favorite overall gaming laptop right now, and it just so happens to be configurable with up to a Nvidia RTX 4090 laptop GPU running at the full 175W potential. The build quality and design and outstanding, there are many configuration options, and the QHD display is a beauty (even if it doesn't have the same pixel density as 4K).

Any of the other options has its own novelty, whether that's mini-LED, liquid cooling, or a thinner and more portable design. There's something for everyone here.

Editor's Choice
Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9
9 / 10
$2699 $3299 Save $600

With a powerful 14th-gen Intel i9 chip, up to an Nvidia RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, and up to 32GB of DDR5 RAM, the Legion Pro 7i all-around extremely capable gaming laptop. Throw in a 240Hz G-SYNC display and you've got a seriously competitive machine.

$3145 at Lenovo $2699 at B&H $3619 at Adorama

Buying a high-end laptop with a Nvidia RTX 4090 graphics card inside generally means that you're aiming for the best performance possible for gaming or for other intensive tasks like 3D rendering. Nvidia's RTX 4090 Laptop GPU won't match the performance of its desktop counterpart due to heat and power constraints, but it will still offer the best mobile power around. If you're interested in taking your gaming PC everywhere and don't want to lag far behind a desktop setup, one of these laptops should be the way to go. I've been sure to specify the RTX 4090's TGP specific to each model to ensure you're not getting a card that's held back by power constraints.

Pros
  • High-end performance that doesn't overheat

  • Many display and config options
Cons
  • Relatively heavy
  • No 4K display option
$3145 at Lenovo $2699 at B&H $3619 at Adorama

When it comes to the overall best gaming laptops available today, the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9 remains our top pick with the 2024 model. It's not absolutely perfect, but it's easy to look past the so-so battery life (it is a gaming laptop after all) and lack of 4K display option when confronted with all the great features. The build quality is outstanding, and the design is fairly subdued with just the right amount of flair. It has a load of ports along the back for easy cable management, there's a 1080p webcam, the speakers offer quality sound, and there's plenty of customizable RGB lighting.

The 14th-gen Intel Core i9-14900HX processor (CPU) has the power to rival a desktop setup, and it's joined by up to 32GB of DDR5 RAM and 2TB of M.2 PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSD storage split between two drives. Opting for an RTX 4090 laptop GPU brings the price up to well over $3,000, but that's got to be expected if you're aiming for this kind of performance. It has a whopping 175W of power, too, the maximum allowed by Nvidia's design.

Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9 review: An already-great laptop gets a spec bump and display upgrade

Related
Alienware x16 R2 review: This gaming laptop is more powerful than ever — most of the time

This gaming laptop is still full of RGB and great aesthetics, but now includes Intel Core Ultra and other upgrades inside.

The 16-inch display comes with a 16:10 aspect ratio and Quad HD+ resolution with a 240Hz refresh rate, or a Full HD+ panel with a 480Hz refresh rate depending on what you want to prioritize. Both panels are pretty good, covering 100% of DCI-P3, and in the words of our reviewer Brady Snyder, "the Alienware x16 R2 can keep up with the most color-accurate gaming laptops out there".

Aside from the essentials, you get plenty of ports for such a thin laptop, plus there's a ton of RGB, with the keyboard, ring light on the back, and even an optional light-up touchpad that's way more flashy than it needed to be. It's really fun laptop to show off, on top of just being a great one.

Best OLED laptop
Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 (2024)

A stunning display in a sleek chassis

8.5 / 10
$3000 $3300 Save $300

The Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 is an even sleeker option if you want a gaming machine that's more portable but still powerful. It has an Intel Core 9 processor and up to an RTX 4090 GPU running at 115W. It's also got a stunning OLED display that's even better than mini-LED,

Pros
  • Stunning OLED display offers absolutely stunning visuals and HDR
  • Thin and relatively light design considering the specs
  • Great speaker setup and a large touchpad
Cons
  • GPU is less powerful than larger laptops
  • It can get pretty hot
$3000 at Best Buy $3500 at Amazon $3300 at Asus

Mini-LED displays are great, but if you want the best visual experience, OLED is the way to go, and Asus is happy to provide with the ROG Zephyrus G16. This 16-inch laptop packs a stunning OLED display with 500 nits of brightness in HDR mode, offering one of the best HDR experiences you can get. The individually-lit pixels are even better than mini-LED, whether you're gaming, working, or watching movies.

It's a pretty fast laptop, too. Due to the thin design, it comes with an Intel Core Ultra 9 185H processor rather than the more power-hungry HX-series chips, but it's still plenty fast. You also get the option for an RTX 4090, though it's limited to 115W of power. This is a consequence of the thinner design, and it's important to note this is the lightest laptop on this list. You lose some performance, but you get some portability in return, so it's all about picking your priorities.

Related
Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 (2024) review: Major redesign makes this laptop great for more than just gaming

The new ROG Zephyrus G16 is a premium laptop with mature design, appealing to anyone who wants to work and game on the same device.

Even with its smaller chassis, the ROG Zephyrus G16 has an excellent keyboard with plenty of travel, a large touchpad, and solid speakers that easily drown out fan noise. You also get a solid selection of ports, and on top of that, it's all wrapped in a very sleek design that barely gives away this is a gaming laptop. This is a laptop you can use anywhere, and as a result, it can be great for almost anyone.

In our review, Cale Hunt said "The ROG Zephryus G16 (2024) is one of the best built laptops I've tested, with a high attention to detail evident everywhere you look", and that's not overselling it. This is a great choice.

Getting the best RTX 4090 laptop for you

Any of the laptops that I've listed above come with an option for the mighty Nvidia RTX 4090 laptop GPU, capable of delivering the best gaming performance possible before stepping into a desktop setup. Many of these laptops will in fact rival and even best some of the most popular pre-built gaming desktop PCs when it comes to raw power, and of course you get a built-in display and many other extra features that make your life with the laptop as easy as possible. None of these laptops are affordable, but that 's to be expected if you want an RTX 4090.

These are all great laptops, but our top pick has to go to the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9. It's our favorite overall gaming laptop right now, and it just so happens to be configurable with up to a Nvidia RTX 4090 laptop GPU running at the full 175W potential. The build quality and design and outstanding, there are many configuration options, and the QHD display is a beauty (even if it doesn't have the same pixel density as 4K).

Any of the other options has its own novelty, whether that's mini-LED, liquid cooling, or a thinner and more portable design. There's something for everyone here.

Editor's Choice
Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 9
9 / 10
$2699 $3299 Save $600

With a powerful 14th-gen Intel i9 chip, up to an Nvidia RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, and up to 32GB of DDR5 RAM, the Legion Pro 7i all-around extremely capable gaming laptop. Throw in a 240Hz G-SYNC display and you've got a seriously competitive machine.

$3145 at Lenovo $2699 at B&H $3619 at Adorama

item.title: How to extract and install a tar.gz file on Ubuntu
item.itunes:title: How to extract and install a tar.gz file on Ubuntu
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
One of the things you'll come across during everyday use of a great Linux distribution like Ubuntu is installing apps and opening certain files. While most apps can be found in the Ubuntu Software Center or even as manual downloads, there might be a time when an app or a file you want will come as a tar.gz file type.

This is an archive of files, almost similar to ZIP on Windows 11 and other operating systems. It combines both the TAR (which is known as tape) archive and the ZIP archive format into one, so the data is compressed and reduced in size. Naturally, you'll need to extract the tar.gz file to install or open whatever is inside. We'll guide you through that today in two easy ways, covering both the GUI and the terminal app.

How to extract a tar.gz archive on Ubuntu using the GUI

You don't need a lot of technical knowledge to open a tar.gz file on Ubuntu. The operating system has native support for opening and managing this file type through the Files app.

  1. Open Files.
  2. Navigate to where the tar.gz archive is saved. (If downloaded from a website using a browser, check the Downloads folder.)
  3. To view what the files are without extracting the archive, right-click on the file and click Open with Archive Manager.
  4. To properly open and install the files, return to the originally downloaded file, right-click, and choose Extract to.
  5. Pick a location.
  6. Click Select.

Once you pick a location, navigate there once the process has completed and you should see a new folder with all the extracted files waiting for you.

How to extract a tar.gz archive on Ubuntu using Terminal

If you're more technical, you can also open and exact a tar.gz file by using the terminal. This is easy, provided you know the path for the downloaded file.

  1. Open Terminal.
  2. Extract the tar.gz archive (replace file.tar.gz with the archive name): 
    tar -xvzf file.tar.gz

If the archive isn't in your current directory, you can use the following command to automatically extract it without moving to the right folder. 

tar -xvzf file.tar.gz -C /path/to/file

Remember to replace file.tar.gz with the archive name and /path/to/file with the correct values. The files will be extracted to the current or specified directory on your Ubuntu install and you can open it to discover the files. For the flags we've used in the above command:

  • -f is specifying the file name.
  • -v is listing the files while extracted.
  • -x means what to extract the files from.
  • -z is what's compressed.

Another simple Linux operation!

As you can tell, it's pretty simple to extract and install tar.gz files on Ubuntu. For most people, the GUI method will do the trick, but if you're up for adventure, it's always great to mess around with the terminal and enter commands.

item.title: 27 years ago, Microsoft bought $150 million worth of Apple stock after the company almost went bankrupt
item.itunes:title: 27 years ago, Microsoft bought $150 million worth of Apple stock after the company almost went bankrupt
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • After losing Steve Jobs in the 80s, Apple faced a slow decline until his return in 1997 saved the company from bankruptcy.
  • Microsoft's $150 million investment in Apple was more about saving itself from monopoly accusations than saving Apple.
  • Lawsuits surrounding copyright infringement and monopolistic practices were settled between Apple and Microsoft in the late 90s.

The early 1990s were not an easy time for Apple. After losing the company's main visionary, Steve Jobs, and falling into decline and disarray, the company was on the verge of financial bankruptcy by the start of 1997. But luckily, due to the return of Apple’s CEO, a settled lawsuit, and a $150 million stock investment by Microsoft, the company was saved from bankruptcy and went on to become the cultural and innovative icon that it is today.

Related
On this day 41 years ago, Apple released the Lisa computer for $10,000

Celebrating the 41 years since Apple's groundbreaking release of the Lisa computer, a technological milestone that helped revolutionize modern PCs

The slow decline of Apple

Apple was Jobless until 1997

Source: Matthew Yohe via Wikimedia Commons

In 1985, Apple would be forever changed as Steve Jobs, the co-creator of Apple, would be let go from the company. After Jobs was replaced as the project lead for the Macintosh computer, he had a clash with the then-board of directors at Apple, which led to his dismissal. Steve Jobs then decided to “borrow” a few Apple employees and founded a new company, NeXT, that developed computers with advanced image software.

This era was not only important for PC history but also for film history. While Steve Jobs was CEO at NeXT the next year in 1986, he would go on to encounter a team working on 3D image and video animation. Jobs would later buy the video animation company so that he could use the 3D animations at NeXT. The company that he bought that day is now known as Pixar.

During this time, Apple continued a slow decline. The company’s Macintosh computer line wasn't selling, the company management was constantly changing, and it didn't help that Apple computers were twice as expensive as Microsoft alternatives. Without Steve Jobs, it didn't seem like Apple was going to make it.

When Jobs finally returned to Apple in July 1997, the company was making pagers and PDA-style devices. Steve Jobs took a good hard look at the company and decided that the company needed a bold shift in vision. In the beginning, he only acted in an advisory role. Only later was he promoted back to CEO of the company in 1997, when he began to make real changes.

One of Steve Jobs's requirements of Apple before he returned as CEO was for the company to acquire and merge with his new company, NeXT. Using the Apple brand and design, in combination with the operating system and GUI software created at NeXT, Jobs would go on to create the original Mac OS X, and start a new line of devices that we now know as the iMac and iPod.

Microsoft didn't save Apple, it was simply saving itself

Copyright infringement, lawsuits, and monopoly

Microsoft is often said to be the only reason why Apple exists today. While this most likely wasn't the case, it is true that Apple was not looking good towards the start of the 21st century. The opposite was true for Microsoft though, who, at the time, controlled around 92% of the PC market.

Between 1996 and 1997, Microsoft was hit with two major lawsuits and had both the public and the government watching the company as it was nearly monopolizing the market. In the early 90s, Apple had hired the San Fransisco Canyon company to create a video editor called QuickTime which would be used for future versions of Macintosh. This code would later be found on Windows operating systems and in Intel drivers. This meant that both Microsoft and Intel were guilty of copyright infringement and software piracy. The lawsuit was passed around for a few years, but the whole deal was ultimately settled outside of court. It is alleged that Microsoft paid at least $100-$200 million in settlement money.

During the same time, the US government had started to notice that Microsoft had a rather large market share in the PC sector, and its actions were putting companies out of business. There was an outcry from one of the largest internet search engines at the time, Netscape, who protested that Microsoft should not be allowed to monopolize the market by bundling Windows with Internet Explorer. Microsoft was offering the browser for free, which was not common at the time, as most internet search engines charged a fee for use. This all seemed to fall into the realm of unfair business practices, so both Netscape and the United States government took them to court. Netscape and other search engines did end up going out of business due to the launch of Internet Explorer.

How did it end?

So, in 1997, Apple was still on the verge of bankruptcy but had won a lawsuit against Microsoft, acquired the NeXT company, and welcomed back its visionary CEO, Steve Jobs. Then later that same year, at the Mac World conference, Steve Jobs stepped out on stage and announced that Apple would start its partnership program with Microsoft, including 5 years of Microsoft Office support for all Mac devices. Somewhere between the lawsuit and the near-bankruptcy, Microsoft also managed to get Apple to ship all next-generation Mac devices with... Internet Explorer.

While Apple probably would have suffered without the financial help of Microsoft when Steve Jobs first arrived back, it wouldn't have failed. That being said, Microsoft was no saint here. For the most part, the company was far more concerned with saving their own neck by propping up a competitor so that the government wouldn't view Microsoft as a monopoly unfairly controlling the market.

item.title: What is Plex Pass and is it worth it?
item.itunes:title: What is Plex Pass and is it worth it?
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • Upgrade to Plex Pass for hardware transcoding, offline media, live TV, and more features.
  • Plex is your all-in-one solution for organizing and streaming all your purchased media.
  • Consider Plex Pass as a support for the platform, with added bonuses for your entertainment arsenal.

Plex Media Server is a popular choice for those who wish to catalog purchased media and stream it to just about anywhere in the world. Plex is free to use but the company offers an option subscription or one-time fee to upgrade called Plex Pass. Although you don't need Plex Pass to enjoy all your media, it may be worth paying for should you require additional features and functionality.

Related
Best NAS for Plex in 2024

Create your very own Spotify and Netflix streaming service.

What is Plex?

Have you ever wondered if there was a way to catalog all your purchased media? I'm talking music, movies, TV shows, and even photos captured on your smartphone(s). That's what Plex Media server is. This powerful software can run on a PC, NAS, or server and catalog all your data. Once everything has been saved into Plex's database, it can then stream everything to your devices anywhere in the world.

Think of Plex as a way to take back control of your media from streaming services such as Spotify, Prime, Netflix, and others. Purchase the media you want to enjoy and let Plex handle the rest.

Related
Best Plex alternatives in 2024: Jellyfin, Emby, and more

If you're looking for an alternative to Plex, there are a few options that are just as good.

What is Plex Pass?

Plex Pass was launched in 2012 to generate revenue for the Plex project without forcing everyone to pay for a commercial license. Three different pricing tiers are available, starting from $5 per month. An annual subscription will save you $20 at $40 per year. Finally, a lifetime pass costs $120 and unlocks all the advantages of Plex Pass for the lifetime of your account.

The free tier of Plex has a few limitations, but there's a good chance you wouldn't even notice through general usage. You don't technically require a Plex Pass to catalog and stream movies, shows, and music you own, but there are several benefits of having an active Plex Pass, especially if you consume a lot of media through your server or NAS.

What upgrades do you get with Plex Pass?

The primary upgrade included with Plex Pass is hardware transcoding. If a file stored on your server, be it a movie or show, isn't supported by the recipient device (your TV, tablet, or PC), the server needs to transcode the file to a format supported by the client. This requires considerable system resources on the server end. With the right CPU, hardware transcoding can speed this process up and reduce overall resource requirements.

Media can be saved to a device, such as your smartphone or tablet. This is invaluable for times when you'll have restricted access to the internet. With Plex Pass, you could download entire music playlists offline using the official PlexAmp app, allowing for smooth playback even through periods of weak cell coverage during a long drive. Other handy features include intro skipping and live TV support.

To recap, here are all of the upgrades offered by a Plex Pass:

  • Hardware transcoding.
  • Offline media support.
  • Intro skipping, trailers, and extras.
  • DVR/live TV functionality.
  • Dedicated PlexAmp music app.

Is Plex Pass worth the cost?

This is a difficult question to answer. Some view Plex Pass as a means to lock away features to enhance the streaming experience. Others see this optional upgrade as a way to support the relatively small company to develop such an important platform. If Plex Media Server is the backbone of your entertainment at home, you may want to consider upgrading to the Plex Pass. The offline media support and PlexAmp alone are great additions to your media arsenal.

If nothing else, consider the unlocked features as a "thank you" for your support.

Plex Pass

Upgrade your media streaming service with Plex Pass for offline playback, hardware transcoding, live TV support, and more.

$120 at Plex
item.description:
Key Takeaways

  • Upgrade to Plex Pass for hardware transcoding, offline media, live TV, and more features.
  • Plex is your all-in-one solution for organizing and streaming all your purchased media.
  • Consider Plex Pass as a support for the platform, with added bonuses for your entertainment arsenal.

Plex Media Server is a popular choice for those who wish to catalog purchased media and stream it to just about anywhere in the world. Plex is free to use but the company offers an option subscription or one-time fee to upgrade called Plex Pass. Although you don't need Plex Pass to enjoy all your media, it may be worth paying for should you require additional features and functionality.

Related
Best NAS for Plex in 2024

Create your very own Spotify and Netflix streaming service.

What is Plex?

Have you ever wondered if there was a way to catalog all your purchased media? I'm talking music, movies, TV shows, and even photos captured on your smartphone(s). That's what Plex Media server is. This powerful software can run on a PC, NAS, or server and catalog all your data. Once everything has been saved into Plex's database, it can then stream everything to your devices anywhere in the world.

Think of Plex as a way to take back control of your media from streaming services such as Spotify, Prime, Netflix, and others. Purchase the media you want to enjoy and let Plex handle the rest.

Related
Best Plex alternatives in 2024: Jellyfin, Emby, and more

If you're looking for an alternative to Plex, there are a few options that are just as good.

What is Plex Pass?

Plex Pass was launched in 2012 to generate revenue for the Plex project without forcing everyone to pay for a commercial license. Three different pricing tiers are available, starting from $5 per month. An annual subscription will save you $20 at $40 per year. Finally, a lifetime pass costs $120 and unlocks all the advantages of Plex Pass for the lifetime of your account.

The free tier of Plex has a few limitations, but there's a good chance you wouldn't even notice through general usage. You don't technically require a Plex Pass to catalog and stream movies, shows, and music you own, but there are several benefits of having an active Plex Pass, especially if you consume a lot of media through your server or NAS.

What upgrades do you get with Plex Pass?

The primary upgrade included with Plex Pass is hardware transcoding. If a file stored on your server, be it a movie or show, isn't supported by the recipient device (your TV, tablet, or PC), the server needs to transcode the file to a format supported by the client. This requires considerable system resources on the server end. With the right CPU, hardware transcoding can speed this process up and reduce overall resource requirements.

Media can be saved to a device, such as your smartphone or tablet. This is invaluable for times when you'll have restricted access to the internet. With Plex Pass, you could download entire music playlists offline using the official PlexAmp app, allowing for smooth playback even through periods of weak cell coverage during a long drive. Other handy features include intro skipping and live TV support.

To recap, here are all of the upgrades offered by a Plex Pass:

  • Hardware transcoding.
  • Offline media support.
  • Intro skipping, trailers, and extras.
  • DVR/live TV functionality.
  • Dedicated PlexAmp music app.

Is Plex Pass worth the cost?

This is a difficult question to answer. Some view Plex Pass as a means to lock away features to enhance the streaming experience. Others see this optional upgrade as a way to support the relatively small company to develop such an important platform. If Plex Media Server is the backbone of your entertainment at home, you may want to consider upgrading to the Plex Pass. The offline media support and PlexAmp alone are great additions to your media arsenal.

If nothing else, consider the unlocked features as a "thank you" for your support.

Plex Pass

Upgrade your media streaming service with Plex Pass for offline playback, hardware transcoding, live TV support, and more.

$120 at Plex

item.title: OneNote vs. Standard Notes: Which note-taking app is better?
item.itunes:title: OneNote vs. Standard Notes: Which note-taking app is better?
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • Standard Notes has better cross-platform availability, including a native app for Linux users.
  • Both OneNote and Standard Notes keep it simple with user interface, but Standard Notes offers more customization options.
  • Standard Notes offers unique features like spreadsheet support and link related notes, while OneNote integrates well with Microsoft 365.

Digital note-taking apps have truly transformed the way we capture thoughts, ideas, tasks, and personal projects. Among the endless capable solutions, OneNote and Standard Notes have grabbed the attention of productivity nerds and power note-takers. They both have their own distinct approach to note-taking, overall organization, and functionality. In this post, we will compare both tools based on the user interface, customization, note-taking tools, features, price, and more.

Related
I don't pay for OneNote, Notion, or Evernote – these are the free options I recommend instead

You can still create a productive workflow without any paid apps or eye-watering subscriptions

19

Cross-platform availability

Standard Notes has a slight edge in terms of cross-platform availability. While both note-taking apps are available on major platforms like iOS, Android, Windows, Mac, web, and as an extension on Chrome Web Store, Standard Notes has a native app on Linux, too. It should come in handy for those who have a Linux machine in their workflow.

User interface and customization

No major surprises here. Unlike some of the new-age note-taking solutions, both OneNote and Standard Notes have kept it simple with UI. All the essential editing tools are at the top, and you can access your notebooks, sub-notebooks, and tags from the sidebar. New users won’t have a hard time getting started with either program.

In terms of customization, you can only switch between a dark and light theme in OneNote. However, Standard Notes is a step ahead with different themes, such as Carbon, Titanium, Dark, Midnight, Futura, and more.

Note-taking tools and organization

Whether you use OneNote or Standard Notes, you have a bunch of tools at your disposal to craft perfect notes. Standard Notes has a super note offering where you can unlock more functions such as Markdown support, collapsible sections, code blocks, and password generation.

In comparison, OneNote offers several page colors and styles to choose from. Students can use it to replicate their physical notebooks in OneNote.

In terms of note organization, both tools are more or less the same. You can create multiple notebooks and subsections, and draft new notes easily. Standard Notes also supports tags (#hashtags) to organize your notes effectively.

Related
You are using OneNote wrong if you haven't tried these templates

Use these templates and create an ideal OneNote page in seconds

OneNote vs. Standard Notes: Features

Let’s go over some of the unique features of OneNote and Standard Notes, beginning with the former.

Microsoft 365 integration

As an important component of the Microsoft 365 ecosystem, OneNote has flawless integration with other company apps like Outlook, OneDrive, Loop, and more. For example, you can insert and edit Loop components, create Outlook tasks, and insert a spreadsheet from Excel right into a OneNote page.

Math equations

This is one of the best and often overlooked features of OneNote. You can simply perform basic math formulas on a OneNote page. You need to type the math equation and press = at the end to get the final result, or write it out with your stylus if you prefer. You don’t need to open the calculator separately to get an answer.

Drawing and hand-writing tools

Students and creatives will find the handwriting and drawing tools quite useful. It supports multiple types of pens, strokes, shades, shapes, and more. Students can change the page style, pick up a stylus, and start writing lecture notes.

Related
How to use the most advanced OneNote features to supercharge your note taking

OneNote is more capable than you may think

Now, let’s go over some of the noteworthy (no pun intended) features of Standard Notes.

Link related notes

You can link related notes right at the top. You can simply start typing a note name and select a relevant option from the drop-down menu. It’s a productivity booster for many pro note-takers, as it helps keep your associated thoughts together.

Spreadsheet support

Source: Standard Notes

Built-in spreadsheet support is quite unusual for a note-taking app. You can switch your note type to a spreadsheet format and create useful databases in no time. It’s entirely up to you to pick whatever note type is most relevant to your needs.

Support for plugins

With a paid plan, you can also unlock more plugins, such as advanced checklists, different themes, and more, for your Standard Notes account.

OneNote vs. Standard Notes: Privacy

Privacy is a core part of Standard Notes. All your notes are end-to-end encrypted, and it supports 2FA to keep prying eyes away from your account. The sharing and export options are quite limited, though.

With OneNote, you can invite others for real-time collaboration and even share an entire notebook with password protection and expiry links. Both apps let you password-protect notes and specific sections.

OneNote vs. Standard Notes: Pricing

Here is where it gets interesting. All OneNote features are completely free to use. You only need to pay for OneDrive storage to store and sync your notes across devices. The pricing starts at $2 per month for 100GB of space.

In contrast, most Standard Notes features are locked behind a paid subscription. The Standard Plus plan is priced at $63 per year. There is no monthly plan.

A note-worthy duel

Whether you are a student managing multiple subjects, or a professional wrangling complex projects, or simply someone who likes to organize their thoughts and ideas effectively, selecting the right note-taking app can make all the difference in your productivity.

While both OneNote and Standard Notes excel at the basics, they have somewhat different approaches. Evaluate your needs, note-taking style, and budget, and pick a suitable option for your workflow.

Joplin is another very capable note-taking tool available. Check out our comparison between OneNote and Joplin to learn all the differences.
